title: Bit Manipulation Basics
description: Understand and apply basic bit manipulation operations: AND, OR, XOR, NOT, left shift, right shift.
hints: AND with 1 checks last bit
Right shift divides by 2
XOR with same number gives 0

title: Check if Bit is Set
description: Check whether the ith bit of a number n is set or not.
hints: Use (n >> i) & 1
Or check if (n & (1 << i)) != 0

title: Set, Clear and Toggle Bits
description: Set, clear, and toggle the ith bit of a number n.
hints: Set: n | (1<<i)
Clear: n & ~(1<<i)
Toggle: n ^ (1<<i)

title: Count Set Bits (Brian Kernighan)
description: Count the number of set bits (1s) in the binary representation of n.
hints: n & (n-1) removes the lowest set bit
Count how many times you can do this
Or use Integer.bitCount(n)

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two.
hints: Powers of 2 have exactly one set bit
n & (n-1) == 0 checks this
Handle n <= 0 case

title: Find the XOR of numbers from L to R
description: Find XOR of all numbers from L to R.
hints: XOR(1..n) follows a pattern: n%4 gives 0:n, 1:1, 2:n+1, 3:0
XOR(L..R) = XOR(1..R) ^ XOR(1..L-1)

title: Single Number
description: Every element appears twice except one. Find the element that appears once.
hints: XOR of same numbers is 0
XOR all elements
The result is the single number

approaches.intuition: For each element, count how many times it appears. Return the one that appears once.
approaches.explanation: For each element, iterate through the array and count its occurrences. If count is 1, return it.

approaches.intuition: XOR of two identical numbers is 0, and XOR with 0 returns the number itself. XOR all elements together and the pairs cancel out, leaving the single element.
approaches.explanation: Initialize result = 0. XOR every element into result. Since a^a=0 and a^0=a, all pairs cancel and only the unique element remains.

title: Two Numbers Appearing Odd Times
description: In an array where every element appears even times except two, find those two elements.
hints: XOR all to get x^y
Find any set bit in x^y
Divide into two groups by that bit and XOR each group

title: Implement Stack using Arrays
description: Implement a Stack data structure using an array with push, pop, peek, and isEmpty operations.
hints: Use a top pointer starting at -1
Push: increment top, arr[top]=val
Pop: return arr[top--]

title: Implement Queue using Arrays
description: Implement a Queue data structure using an array with enqueue, dequeue, and peek operations.
hints: Track front and rear pointers
Use circular queue to avoid wasted space

title: Implement Stack using Queue
description: Implement a stack using a single queue.
hints: On push, rotate the queue so new element is at front
This makes pop O(1)

title: Implement Queue using Stack
description: Implement a FIFO queue using two stacks.
hints: Use two stacks: input and output
Transfer from input to output only when output is empty

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing '(', ')', '{', '}', '[', ']', determine if the input string is valid.
hints: Push opening brackets on stack
On closing bracket, check if top matches
Stack must be empty at end

approaches.intuition: Repeatedly replace matched pairs () [] {} with empty string until no more replacements happen. If the string becomes empty, it is valid.
approaches.explanation: Keep calling String.replace() on matched pairs until the string stops changing. Check if the result is empty.

approaches.intuition: Use a stack to track unmatched opening brackets. When a closing bracket is encountered, the top of the stack must be its matching open bracket.
approaches.explanation: Iterate each character. Push opening brackets. For closing brackets, pop the top and verify it matches. If not, return false. At the end, the stack must be empty.

title: Next Greater Element
description: For each element in array, find the next greater element to its right. Return -1 if none exists.
hints: Use a monotonic decreasing stack
Process right to left
While stack top <= current, pop; answer is stack top or -1

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Find the area of the largest rectangle in a histogram.
hints: For each bar, find left and right boundaries where height < current
Use monotonic stack to find these in O(n)
Area = height * (right - left - 1)

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure that follows the Least Recently Used (LRU) cache constraint.

examples.input: capacity=2, put(1,1), put(2,2), get(1), put(3,3), get(2)

hints: Use HashMap + Doubly Linked List
HashMap for O(1) lookup, DLL for O(1) insert/delete
Move accessed node to front, evict from back

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an array nums, there is a sliding window of size k. Find the maximum in each window.
hints: Use a monotonic deque
Deque stores indices in decreasing order of values
Remove elements outside the window from front

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
hints: Use sliding window with a HashSet
Expand right, shrink left on duplicate
Track max length

approaches.intuition: Generate all substrings and check each one for repeating characters. Return the length of the longest one that has no repeats.
approaches.explanation: Use two nested loops to generate every substring. For each substring, use a HashSet to check for duplicates. Track the maximum valid length.

approaches.intuition: Use two pointers (left, right) to maintain a window with no repeating chars. Expand right; when a duplicate is found, shrink from left until the duplicate is removed.
approaches.explanation: Use left and right pointers and a HashSet. Move right forward, adding chars to the set. When a duplicate is found, move left forward and remove chars until the duplicate is gone.

approaches.intuition: Store the last seen index of each character in a map. When a duplicate is found, jump left directly to lastIndex+1 instead of sliding one by one.
approaches.explanation: Use a HashMap to store the latest index of each character. When the current char was seen at position p, set left = max(left, p+1). This avoids the inner while loop.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Find the minimum window in s which contains all characters of t.
hints: Use frequency maps for t and current window
Track how many characters are satisfied
Shrink left when window is valid

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given n non-negative integers representing elevation map, compute how much water can be trapped.
hints: Water at index i = min(maxLeft, maxRight) - height[i]
Two pointer: compare left and right maxima
Move pointer from the smaller max side

title: Container With Most Water
description: Find two lines that form a container that holds the most water.
hints: Use two pointers at both ends
Area = min(height[l], height[r]) * (r - l)
Move the pointer with smaller height inward

title: Subarray with Given Sum (Positive Numbers)
description: Find a contiguous subarray with sum equal to k. Return start and end indices.
hints: Use sliding window since all positive
Expand right when sum < k, shrink left when sum > k

title: Kth Largest Element in Array
description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array.
hints: Use a min-heap of size k
If heap size > k, poll the minimum
Top of heap is kth largest

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array, return the k most frequent elements.
hints: Build frequency map
Use min-heap of size k by frequency
Or use bucket sort for O(n)

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: Merge k sorted linked lists and return it as one sorted list.
hints: Use a min-heap with one node from each list
Poll minimum, add its next to heap
Or divide and conquer: merge pairs

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Design a data structure to find the median from a stream of numbers.
hints: Use two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half
Keep sizes balanced (differ by at most 1)
Median is top of larger heap or average of both tops

title: Task Scheduler
description: Given tasks and cooldown n, find minimum time to finish all tasks.
hints: Always execute most frequent task first
Use max-heap + cooldown queue
Result = max(tasks.length, (maxFreq-1)*(n+1)+count of max freq tasks)

title: Activity Selection Problem
description: Select maximum number of activities that can be performed in a single time frame without overlap.
hints: Sort by finish time
Always pick the activity that finishes earliest
Greedy: pick if start >= last finish

title: Fractional Knapsack
description: Given items with weights and values, maximize value in a knapsack of capacity W. Fractions allowed.
hints: Sort by value/weight ratio descending
Take items with best ratio first
Take fraction of last item if needed

title: Jump Game
description: Given an array of non-negative integers, can you reach the last index?
hints: Track maximum reachable index
If i > maxReach, return false
Update maxReach = max(maxReach, i + nums[i])

title: Jump Game II
description: Find the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index.
hints: Track current jump boundary and max reachable
When i reaches boundary, increment jumps and update boundary to maxReach

title: Minimum Platforms
description: Find minimum number of platforms required at a railway station.
hints: Sort arrival and departure separately
Use two pointer technique
Count trains at station at same time

title: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
description: Return the inorder traversal (Left, Root, Right) of a binary tree's values.
hints: Recursive: left, root, right
Iterative: use stack, push left while not null, process node, go right

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Return the level order traversal of a binary tree (BFS).
hints: Use a queue for BFS
Track level size at each iteration
Process all nodes at current level

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Find the maximum depth (height) of a binary tree.
hints: Recursive: 1 + max(left depth, right depth)
Base case: null node returns 0

title: Diameter of Binary Tree
description: Find the length of the diameter of a binary tree (longest path between any two nodes).
hints: Diameter through root = left height + right height
Track global max during height calculation
Don't compute height and diameter separately

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of Binary Tree
description: Find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two nodes p and q in a binary tree.
hints: If root is null or root is p or q, return root
Recurse on left and right
If both non-null, root is LCA; else return non-null side

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
description: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree. Path can start and end at any node.
hints: At each node, max path = max(left, 0) + val + max(right, 0)
Update global max with this path
Return val + max(left, right, 0) for parent

title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder
description: Construct a binary tree from preorder and inorder traversal arrays.
hints: First element of preorder is root
Find root in inorder to split left/right subtrees
Use HashMap for O(1) index lookup

title: Search in BST
description: Given the root of a BST and an integer val, find the node with value equal to val.
hints: If val < root, go left; if val > root, go right
Iterative is O(h) space efficient

title: Insert into BST
description: Insert a value into a BST and return the root of the updated tree.
hints: Navigate like search to find insertion point
Create new node at null position

title: Delete Node in BST
description: Delete a node with given key from a BST and return the root.
hints: Navigate to find node
Case 1: leaf node - delete directly
Case 2: one child - replace with child
Case 3: two children - replace with inorder successor

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of BST
description: Find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes in a BST.
hints: If both p,q < root, LCA is in left subtree
If both p,q > root, LCA is in right subtree
Otherwise root is the LCA

title: Kth Smallest Element in BST
description: Find the kth smallest element in a BST.
hints: Inorder traversal of BST gives sorted order
Count nodes during inorder, return at kth

title: Number of Provinces (Graph Connected Components)
description: Find the number of provinces (connected components) in an undirected graph.
hints: DFS/BFS from unvisited nodes
Each DFS call = one province
Or use Union-Find

title: BFS of Graph
description: Given an undirected graph, print a BFS traversal starting from vertex 0.
hints: Use a queue and visited array
Enqueue neighbors of each dequeued node
Process level by level

title: DFS of Graph
description: Given an undirected graph, print a DFS traversal starting from vertex 0.
hints: Use recursion or explicit stack
Mark visited before recursing
Order depends on adjacency list order

title: Detect Cycle in Undirected Graph
description: Detect if there is a cycle in an undirected graph.
hints: BFS: track parent, if visited neighbor is not parent, cycle exists
Or use DFS with parent tracking
Or Union-Find: if same component when adding edge

title: Topological Sort (BFS - Kahn's Algorithm)
description: Return topological ordering of a DAG using Kahn's BFS algorithm.
hints: Compute in-degree of each node
Add 0 in-degree nodes to queue
Process each node, reduce in-degree of neighbors; add when 0

title: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
description: Find the shortest path from source to all vertices in a weighted graph.
hints: Use min-heap with (distance, node)
Relax edges of dequeued node
Skip if already processed with shorter distance

title: Climbing Stairs
description: Count distinct ways to climb n stairs taking 1 or 2 steps at a time.
hints: dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]
Base cases: dp[1]=1, dp[2]=2
This is Fibonacci!

approaches.intuition: At every step we can take 1 or 2 stairs. Recursively count ways from the top down. This has overlapping subproblems.
approaches.explanation: climbStairs(n) = climbStairs(n-1) + climbStairs(n-2), base cases n=1 → 1, n=2 → 2. Exponential due to recomputation.

approaches.intuition: Cache computed results so each subproblem is solved only once.
approaches.explanation: Add a memo array of size n+1. Before computing, check if result is already cached. If yes, return it. Otherwise compute and store it.

approaches.intuition: Since each state only depends on the previous two states, we only need two variables instead of an array.
approaches.explanation: Keep two variables prev1 and prev2. For each step from 3 to n, compute current = prev1 + prev2, then shift variables forward.

title: Frog Jump (Min Cost)
description: A frog at step 1 wants to reach step n. It can jump 1 or 2 steps. Cost = |height[i] - height[j]|. Find minimum cost.
hints: Optimize to O(1) space

title: House Robber
description: Rob houses without robbing adjacent ones. Maximize total stolen amount.
hints: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2]+nums[i])
Optimize to two variables prev1, prev2

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Find the length of the longest common subsequence of two strings.
hints: dp[i][j] = length of LCS of text1[0..i-1] and text2[0..j-1]
If chars match: dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i-1][j-1]
Else: max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])

title: 0/1 Knapsack
description: Given weights and values, maximize value in a knapsack of capacity W. Each item is used at most once.
hints: dp[i][w] = max value using first i items with capacity w
If item fits: max(dp[i-1][w], val[i]+dp[i-1][w-wt[i]])
Else: dp[i-1][w]

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
hints: O(n^2) DP: dp[i] = 1 + max(dp[j]) for j < i where nums[j] < nums[i]
O(n log n): Binary search on maintained sorted subsequence

title: Edit Distance
description: Find minimum operations (insert, delete, replace) to convert word1 to word2.
hints: dp[i][j] = min operations for word1[0..i-1] to word2[0..j-1]
If chars match: dp[i-1][j-1]
Else: 1 + min(insert: dp[i][j-1], delete: dp[i-1][j], replace: dp[i-1][j-1])

title: Coin Change
description: Find the minimum number of coins that make up the given amount.
hints: dp[i] = min coins to make amount i
dp[0]=0, dp[i] = 1 + min(dp[i-coin]) for each coin <= i

title: Implement Trie
description: Implement a Trie with insert, search, and startsWith methods.
hints: Each TrieNode has children[26] and isEnd flag
Insert: create nodes for each character
Search: traverse and check isEnd; startsWith: just traverse

title: Word Search II
description: Find all words from a given list that exist in a 2D board.
hints: Build Trie from words
DFS from each cell, match against Trie
Mark visited, unmark on backtrack

title: Count of Distinct Substrings
description: Given a string, count the number of distinct substrings using a Trie.
hints: Insert all suffixes into Trie
Each new node created = one new distinct substring
Count = 1 + total new nodes created

title: Minimum Characters to Make String K-Periodic
description: Find the minimum number of character replacements to make string k-periodic.
hints: Group characters by position mod k
For each group, find most frequent character
Changes = group size - max frequency

title: Z-Function (Z-Algorithm)
description: Compute the Z-array where Z[i] is the length of the longest substring starting at i that is also a prefix of the string.
hints: Maintain a Z-box [l, r] for the rightmost matching window
Reuse previous Z values within the Z-box
O(n) time

title: KMP Algorithm
description: Implement the KMP string matching algorithm to find all occurrences of pattern in text.
hints: Build LPS (longest proper prefix which is also suffix) array
Use LPS to skip unnecessary comparisons
O(n+m) time

title: Rabin Karp Algorithm
description: Find all occurrences of a pattern in a text using rolling hash.
hints: Compute rolling hash of pattern and first window
Slide window: subtract old char, add new char hash
On hash match, verify actual string equality

title: Minimum Spanning Tree (Prim's)
description: Find the minimum spanning tree of a weighted undirected graph using Prim's algorithm.
hints: Use min-heap with (weight, node)
Always pick minimum weight edge to unvisited node
Sum of picked edges = MST weight

title: Bellman Ford Algorithm
description: Find shortest paths from source to all vertices, handling negative weight edges.

examples.input: V=5, edges from 0, check negative cycle
examples.output: distances array or -1 if negative cycle

hints: Relax all edges V-1 times
If still relaxable on Vth iteration, negative cycle exists
Works with negative weights, unlike Dijkstra

title: Word Break
description: Given a string s and a dictionary wordDict, return true if s can be segmented into space-separated dictionary words.
hints: dp[i] = can first i characters be segmented
For each i, try all j < i: if dp[j] and s[j..i] in dict, dp[i]=true

title: Burst Balloons
description: Burst balloons to maximize coins. Bursting balloon i gives nums[left]*nums[i]*nums[right] coins.
hints: Think in reverse: which balloon is burst LAST in interval [l,r]
dp[l][r] = max coins from [l,r]
dp[l][r] = max over k: dp[l][k-1] + nums[l-1]*nums[k]*nums[r+1] + dp[k+1][r]
